title: The Eye That Sees Everything But Understands Nothing
script: This creature packs 16 types of color receptors in its eyes — and still sees worse than you. The mantis shrimp holds the most complex eyes on Earth: detecting ultraviolet, infrared, and polarized light simultaneously. Humans run on three receptors. This thing has sixteen. But here's the strange part — it can't distinguish between similar shades as well as we can. All that hardware, and the brain can't fully process it. Send this to someone who thinks more is always better.
search_summary: Mantis shrimp has 16 color receptors but perceives color less accurately than humans with only 3 receptors.
comment_question: Would you trade sharper color vision for 16 types of light detection? 🦐

title: The Tiny Thing Keeping You Alive Right Now
script: Half the air you're breathing right now doesn't come from any forest. It comes from the ocean — specifically, a bacterium called Prochlorococcus, discovered in 1986. One million of them fit inside a single drop of water. Yet they produce more oxygen than the entire Amazon rainforest. But here's the wild part — it's not a plant. It's bacteria. And if it vanishes, so do we. Send this to someone who thinks trees are doing all the work. And this is how.
search_summary: Prochlorococcus bacteria discovered in 1986 produces 50% of Earth's oxygen, more than the Amazon rainforest
comment_question: So the Amazon isn't actually Earth's lungs — a microscopic bacteria is? 👀
